ziegler_nichols.name: Ziegler-Nichols
ziegler_nichols.description: Classic Ziegler-Nichols process reaction rule. Quarter decay ratio response.
ziegler_nichols.unique_feature: Industry standard baseline; quarter decay ratio target; taum/Tm <= 1

cohen_coon.name: Cohen-Coon
cohen_coon.description: Cohen-Coon process reaction method. Extends ZN for taum/Tm between 1.0 and 2.0.
cohen_coon.unique_feature: Extends ZN into high dead-time region (1.0 < taum/Tm <= 2.0); corrects for large delay ratios

zhuang_atherton.name: Zhuang & Atherton (Fast)
zhuang_atherton.description: Zhuang & Atherton minimum ISE regulator. Optimised for fast disturbance rejection.
zhuang_atherton.unique_feature: Minimises ISE for regulatory (load disturbance) response; power-law formula; Zhuang & Atherton 1993

rovira.name: Rovira (Smooth)
rovira.description: Rovira minimum IAE servo tuning. Smooth setpoint tracking.
rovira.unique_feature: Minimises IAE for servo setpoint tracking; balanced smooth response; Rovira 1969 benchmark

hazebroek.name: Hazebroek & Van der Waerden (Disturbance)
hazebroek.description: Hazebroek & Van der Waerden min ISE regulator with tabulated alpha/beta coefficients.
hazebroek.unique_feature: Graphical/tabular derivation; wide taum/Tm range (0.2-5.0); alpha-beta lookup table method

chien_regulator_0os.name: Chien et al. - Regulator 0% Overshoot
chien_regulator_0os.description: Chien et al. regulator for zero overshoot. Conservative disturbance rejection with no oscillation.
chien_regulator_0os.unique_feature: Guarantees 0% overshoot regulatory mode; explicit OS specification; Chien, Hrones & Reswick 1952

chien_regulator_20os.name: Chien et al. - Regulator 20% Overshoot
chien_regulator_20os.description: Chien et al. regulator 20% overshoot. Faster disturbance recovery accepting some oscillation.
chien_regulator_20os.unique_feature: Explicit 20% overshoot regulatory mode; faster than 0% OS; Ti = 2.33*taum

chien_servo_0os.name: Chien et al. - Servo 0% Overshoot
chien_servo_0os.description: Chien et al. servo 0% overshoot. Very conservative smooth setpoint tracking.
chien_servo_0os.unique_feature: Guarantees 0% overshoot for setpoint steps; most conservative Chien variant; Ti = 1.17*Tm

chien_servo_20os.name: Chien et al. - Servo 20% Overshoot
chien_servo_20os.description: Chien et al. servo 20% overshoot. Faster setpoint tracking with moderate oscillation.
chien_servo_20os.unique_feature: Explicit 20% overshoot servo mode; Ti = Tm directly; Chien, Hrones & Reswick 1952

murrill_servo.name: Murrill - Quarter Decay Ratio Servo
murrill_servo.description: Murrill servo targeting quarter decay ratio. Power-law regression formula.
murrill_servo.unique_feature: Power-law regression (non-linear formula); quarter decay ratio servo target; Murrill 1967 thesis

astrom_regulator_20os.name: Astrom & Hagglund - Regulator 20% OS
astrom_regulator_20os.description: Astrom & Hagglund textbook regulator for 20% overshoot (page 150).
astrom_regulator_20os.unique_feature: From Astrom & Hagglund PID Controllers textbook p.150; slightly different Ti from Chien 20% OS

st_clair.name: St. Clair - Conservative PI
st_clair.description: St. Clair conservative tuning for Tm/taum <= 3. Very stable and slow.
st_clair.unique_feature: Very conservative; Ti = Tm exactly; valid for Tm/taum <= 3; St. Clair 1953

rovira_iae_servo.name: Rovira - Minimum IAE Servo
rovira_iae_servo.description: Rovira minimum IAE servo. Optimised for smooth setpoint tracking minimising absolute error.
rovira_iae_servo.unique_feature: Minimises IAE for servo mode; different coefficients from regulator; Rovira et al. 1969

rovira_itae_servo.name: Rovira - Minimum ITAE Servo
rovira_itae_servo.description: Rovira minimum ITAE servo. Penalises long-lasting errors - slowest but smoothest.
rovira_itae_servo.unique_feature: ITAE penalises persistent errors more than IAE/ISE; slowest servo rule; best for noise-sensitive processes

murrill_iae_regulator.name: Murrill - Minimum IAE Regulator
murrill_iae_regulator.description: Murrill minimum IAE regulator. Power-law for optimal load rejection.
murrill_iae_regulator.unique_feature: Minimises IAE for load disturbance rejection; power-law regression; Murrill 1967 doctoral thesis

murrill_ise_regulator.name: Murrill - Minimum ISE Regulator
murrill_ise_regulator.description: Murrill minimum ISE regulator. Penalises large errors heavily - aggressive with more overshoot.
murrill_ise_regulator.unique_feature: ISE penalises large errors more; fastest initial disturbance response; highest overshoot; Murrill 1967

murrill_itae_regulator.name: Murrill - Minimum ITAE Regulator
murrill_itae_regulator.description: Murrill minimum ITAE regulator. Best when long-lasting deviations are costly.
murrill_itae_regulator.unique_feature: ITAE time-weighted penalty; discourages long settling; Murrill 1967; complements ISE/IAE variants

zhuang_ise_servo.name: Zhuang & Atherton - Minimum ISE Servo
zhuang_ise_servo.description: Zhuang & Atherton minimum ISE servo. Fastest setpoint tracking at cost of higher overshoot.
zhuang_ise_servo.unique_feature: ISE servo optimisation; highest gain servo rule; trades overshoot for speed; Zhuang & Atherton 1993

zhuang_istse_regulator.name: Zhuang & Atherton - Minimum ISTSE Regulator
zhuang_istse_regulator.description: Zhuang & Atherton minimum ISTSE (t^2*e^2 integral) regulator.
zhuang_istse_regulator.unique_feature: ISTSE = ISE weighted by t^2; penalises very late-occurring errors; targets tail-end performance specifically

zhuang_istes_regulator.name: Zhuang & Atherton - Minimum ISTES Regulator
zhuang_istes_regulator.description: Zhuang & Atherton minimum ISTES (t*e^2 integral) regulator.
zhuang_istes_regulator.unique_feature: Unique ISTES composite time-error criterion; balances speed and smoothness; Zhuang & Atherton 1993

zhuang_istse_servo.name: Zhuang & Atherton - Minimum ISTSE Servo
zhuang_istse_servo.unique_feature: ISTSE servo variant; Ti linear function of ratio; different from ISTSE regulator; Zhuang 1993

zhuang_istes_servo.name: Zhuang & Atherton - Minimum ISTES Servo
zhuang_istes_servo.description: Zhuang & Atherton minimum ISTES servo. Smoothest composite-criterion servo.
zhuang_istes_servo.unique_feature: ISTES servo - lowest gain Zhuang servo variant; slowest but most damped; no overshoot by design

shinskey_iae_02.name: Shinskey - Min IAE Regulator (taum/Tm=0.2)
shinskey_iae_02.description: Shinskey tabulated minimum IAE regulator for taum/Tm = 0.2.
shinskey_iae_02.unique_feature: Shinskey tabulated values for specific ratio; for taum/Tm=0.2; from Shinskey textbook p.123

haalman.name: Haalman - Direct Synthesis (Ms=1.9)
haalman.description: Haalman direct synthesis with sensitivity Ms=1.9. Simple 2/3 gain formula.
haalman.unique_feature: Derived by specifying Ms=1.9 sensitivity; Ti=Tm exactly; simple clean formula; Haalman 1965

chen_yang.name: Chen & Yang - Direct Synthesis (Ms=1.26)
chen_yang.description: Chen & Yang direct synthesis. Achieves Ms=1.26, Am=2.24, PM=50 degrees.
chen_yang.unique_feature: Targets Ms=1.26, Am=2.24, PM=50 deg; Method 25 parameter estimation; Chen & Yang 1995

smith_corripio_regulator.name: Smith & Corripio - Direct Synthesis Regulator
smith_corripio_regulator.description: Smith & Corripio direct synthesis min IAE regulator. Ti = Tm.
smith_corripio_regulator.unique_feature: Pure DS: Ti=Tm, Kc=Tm/(Km*taum); min IAE target; from Smith & Corripio textbook p.343-346

smith_corripio_servo.name: Smith & Corripio - Direct Synthesis Servo
smith_corripio_servo.description: Smith & Corripio DS servo. Detuned gain (3/5 factor) for smoother setpoint response.
smith_corripio_servo.unique_feature: Servo DS: Kc reduced by 3/5 vs regulator version; targets min IAE for setpoint changes; Ti=Tm

miluse_0os.name: Miluse et al. - 0% Overshoot
miluse_0os.description: Miluse et al. direct synthesis guaranteed 0% overshoot.
miluse_0os.unique_feature: Rigorously guarantees 0% overshoot; Ti=Tm; Method 26 estimation; Miluse et al. 1994

miluse_5os.name: Miluse et al. - 5% Overshoot
miluse_5os.description: Miluse et al. direct synthesis for exactly 5% overshoot.
miluse_5os.unique_feature: Precisely 5% overshoot; Ti=Tm; widest overshoot-selection family (0-50%) of any PI set

miluse_10os.name: Miluse et al. - 10% Overshoot
miluse_10os.description: Miluse et al. for exactly 10% overshoot.
miluse_10os.unique_feature: Precisely 10% overshoot; Ti=Tm constant across all Miluse OS variants; Miluse 1994

miluse_20os.name: Miluse et al. - 20% Overshoot
miluse_20os.description: Miluse et al. for 20% overshoot servo response.
miluse_20os.unique_feature: 20% overshoot; Ti=Tm; Miluse family offers 0-50% OS selection - broadest range of any PI family

miluse_30os.name: Miluse et al. - 30% Overshoot
miluse_30os.description: Miluse et al. for 30% overshoot. Aggressive fast servo response.
miluse_30os.unique_feature: 30% overshoot; fastest Miluse variant; Ti=Tm; for processes where speed is critical

hang_gm15.name: Hang et al. - GM=1.5 (PM=30 deg)
hang_gm15.description: Hang et al. for Gain Margin=1.5, PM=30 degrees. Aggressive with specified stability margins.
hang_gm15.unique_feature: Explicit GM=1.5, PM=30 deg; relay-based ID (Method 11 or 14); fastest Hang variant

hang_gm2.name: Hang et al. - GM=2 (PM=45 deg)
hang_gm2.description: Hang et al. for GM=2, PM=45 degrees. Standard engineering robustness target.
hang_gm2.unique_feature: GM=2, PM=45 deg - standard robustness target; relay autotuning ID; Hang et al. 1991

hang_gm3.name: Hang et al. - GM=3 (PM=60 deg)
hang_gm3.description: Hang et al. for GM=3, PM=60 degrees. Robust with good stability margins.
hang_gm3.unique_feature: GM=3, PM=60 deg; conservative gain; for processes with model uncertainty; Hang 1991

hang_gm4.name: Hang et al. - GM=4 (PM=67.5 deg)
hang_gm4.description: Hang et al. for very high robustness: GM=4, PM=67.5 degrees.
hang_gm4.unique_feature: GM=4, PM=67.5 deg; near-maximum achievable PI robustness; for highly uncertain processes

astrom_honeywell.name: Astrom et al. - Honeywell UDC 6000
astrom_honeywell.description: Astrom et al. rule embedded in Honeywell UDC 6000 industrial autotuner.
astrom_honeywell.unique_feature: Embedded in real Honeywell UDC 6000 autotuner; Method 22 relay+closed-loop identification

davydov_ds.name: Davydov et al. - Direct Synthesis (Damping=0.9)
davydov_ds.description: Davydov et al. DS for closed-loop damping factor = 0.9.
davydov_ds.unique_feature: Damping=0.9 specification; Method 12 tangent-point estimation; Ti is linear function of Tm/taum

bi_et_al.name: Bi et al. - Direct Synthesis
bi_et_al.description: Bi et al. direct synthesis PI using Method 20 closed-loop identification.
bi_et_al.unique_feature: Uses closed-loop step response identification (Method 20); Ti=Tm; Bi, Cai, Wang 1999

smith_delay_dominant.name: Smith - 6dB Gain Margin (Delay Dominant)
smith_delay_dominant.description: Smith DS for delay-dominant processes. 6 dB gain margin. Kc independent of Tm.
smith_delay_dominant.unique_feature: For delay-dominant processes (taum >> Tm); 6 dB gain margin; Kc = 0.35/Km only; Smith 1957

brambilla_robust.name: Brambilla et al. - Robust (<5% OS)
brambilla_robust.description: Brambilla et al. robust design guaranteed <5% overshoot with no model uncertainty (lambda=1).
brambilla_robust.unique_feature: Guarantees <5% overshoot with no model errors; lambda=1; explicitly robust against parameter variation

rivera_imc.name: Rivera et al. - IMC Robust PI
rivera_imc.description: Rivera et al. Internal Model Control (IMC) robust PI. Lambda >= 1.7*taum.
rivera_imc.unique_feature: IMC-based derivation; lambda=1.7*taum adjustable closed-loop time constant; Rivera 1986 industry standard

chien_imc.name: Chien - IMC Robust PI (lambda=Tm)
chien_imc.description: Chien robust IMC PI with lambda=Tm. Conservative, stable, no overshoot.
chien_imc.unique_feature: IMC with lambda=Tm (natural process time constant); guaranteed stability; Chien 1988; safety-critical loops

fruehauf_robust_low.name: Fruehauf et al. - Robust Low Dead-Time
fruehauf_robust_low.description: Fruehauf et al. robust for lag-dominant processes (taum/Tm < 0.33).
fruehauf_robust_low.unique_feature: Two-regime rule: for taum/Tm < 0.33; Ti=5*taum; explicitly handles dead-time dominance transition

fruehauf_robust_high.name: Fruehauf et al. - Robust High Dead-Time
fruehauf_robust_high.description: Fruehauf et al. robust for dead-time-significant processes (taum/Tm >= 0.33).
fruehauf_robust_high.unique_feature: High DT regime; Ti=Tm; conservative; for delay-dominant processes; Fruehauf et al. 1993

chen_robust_ms100.name: Chen et al. - Robust Ms=1.00 (AM=3.14, PM=61.4)
chen_robust_ms100.description: Chen et al. Ms=1.00 - no resonance peak. Maximum stability: AM=3.14, PM=61.4 deg.
chen_robust_ms100.unique_feature: Ms=1.00 means no frequency amplification; maximum robustness; 6 Ms settings (1.0-1.5); Chen 1995

chen_robust_ms120.name: Chen et al. - Robust Ms=1.20 (AM=2.34, PM=51.6)
chen_robust_ms120.description: Chen et al. Ms=1.20. Standard industrial robustness: AM=2.34, PM=51.6 deg.
chen_robust_ms120.unique_feature: Ms=1.2 - standard industrial robustness target; AM=2.34, PM=51.6 deg; best speed-stability balance

chen_robust_ms150.name: Chen et al. - Robust Ms=1.50 (AM=1.96, PM=44.1)
chen_robust_ms150.description: Chen et al. Ms=1.50. More aggressive: AM=1.96, PM=44.1 deg. Faster but less robust.
chen_robust_ms150.unique_feature: Ms=1.5 acceptable limit; fastest Chen robust variant; AM=1.96, PM=44.1 deg; Chen 1995

lee_robust.name: Lee et al. - Desired CL Response (lambda=0.333*taum)
lee_robust.description: Lee et al. targeting CL response e^(-taum*s)/(lambda*s+1), lambda=0.333*taum.
lee_robust.unique_feature: Specifies exact desired closed-loop TF; lambda=0.333*taum; requires Tm > taum; Lee et al. 1996

isaksson_graebe.name: Isaksson & Graebe - Robust PI (lambda=0.4*Tm)
isaksson_graebe.description: Isaksson & Graebe robust PI with lambda=0.4*Tm. Systematic stability-guaranteed design.
isaksson_graebe.unique_feature: Lambda=0.4*Tm; requires Tm > taum; systematic robust design; Isaksson & Graebe 1999

chun_robust_20pct.name: Chun et al. - Robust 20% Parameter Uncertainty
chun_robust_20pct.description: Chun et al. robust PI for 20% uncertainty in all process parameters. Lambda=0.4*Tm.
chun_robust_20pct.unique_feature: Explicit 20% parametric uncertainty specification; lambda=0.4*Tm; Method 21 regression; Chun 1999

voda_landau_pm60.name: Voda & Landau - Symmetrical Optimum (PM=60 deg)
voda_landau_pm60.description: Voda & Landau symmetrical optimum for 60 degree phase margin.
voda_landau_pm60.unique_feature: Symmetrical optimum principle; frequency domain using G_p(j*omega_135) data; PM=60 deg; Voda & Landau 1995
